content:
- Finished the stats aggregation task implementation and passed the required gates: make check, make lint, and make test.
- Updated the task acceptance checkboxes/status, marked passes true, and ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully.
- Next immediate step is only repository hygiene: git add all changes including Ralph files, commit with the required task-finished message including test evidence, push, and stop.

- Started Choose-Task turn.
- Read prior progress log entry: previous turn completed stats aggregation task and task switch.
- Loaded list-active-tasks skill to follow Ralph task ordering from generated summaries.
- Next: refresh task summary and inspect current_tasks/current_tasks_done plus choose-task rules before selecting the next path.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and read current_tasks/current_tasks_done plus choose-task rules.
- Found no active bug tasks and no meta-task candidates; the first unblocked failing task is story-04 observability task-02 because task-01 is already passes=true in done summary.
- Next: write .ralph/current_task.txt to .ralph/tasks/story-04-observability/task-02-prometheus-metrics.md and stop.

- Created .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-04-observability/task-02-prometheus-metrics.md as the next task because it is the earliest unblocked failing non-bug task; its blocker task-01 is already passes=true.
- If resumed, proceed with that task and keep make check, make test, and make lint green.

- Read prior Ralph progress, loaded required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills, and confirmed current task path is .ralph/tasks/story-04-observability/task-02-prometheus-metrics.md.
- Next I am reading the task file and story directory to determine whether this turn is planning-only (TO BE VERIFIED / empty) or execution (NOW EXECUTE).

- Read the current metrics task and confirmed it is not yet marked for execution, so this turn must produce a plan file and stop.
- Inspected benchmarkrun, benchrunner stats, and httpserver boundaries. Likely boundary issue for this task: avoid putting Prometheus string formatting or label policy into the HTTP handler; keep transport thin and move exposition logic behind a typed benchmarkrun/metrics boundary.
- Next I am checking dependencies and test helpers so the plan can specify the likely implementation seam and the first TDD slices.

- Created the planning artifacts for task-02: a TDD-first Prometheus metrics plan ending in TO BE VERIFIED, a smells record for the transport/stats boundary issue, and a <plan> pointer in the task file.
- The plan chooses a typed benchmarkrun metrics snapshot plus writer-style exposition boundary so /metrics does not scrape JSON structs or assemble metric names inside httpserver.
- Stop here now. Next turn should review the plan, switch TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE only if the interface/boundary still looks right, and then quit immediately per Ralph rules.

- Validated task-02 plan against current code. The existing seams are clean: httpserver has a narrow Benchmark interface, benchmarkrun owns public state/results, and benchrunner owns the live histogram accounting.
- Confirmed the planned boundary still makes sense: add a dedicated metrics snapshot/rendering contract in benchmarkrun rather than scraping Results/Stats inside httpserver or leaking JSON/latest-error semantics into Prometheus.
- Next step in this turn is only to mark the plan NOW EXECUTE and quit immediately, so the following turn can start the red-green implementation loop.

- Switched .ralph/tasks/story-04-observability/task-02-prometheus-metrics_plans/2026-04-30-prometheus-metrics-plan.md from TO BE VERIFIED to NOW EXECUTE after validating the interface and boundary against the current code.
- Did not implement anything else this turn, per Ralph rules: plan verification and execution must stay split across turns.
- Next turn should read the NOW EXECUTE plan, follow strict vertical TDD slices, keep Prometheus rendering out of httpserver, and run make check/make lint/make test before finishing.

- Read Ralph progress, current task pointer, and the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill files.
- Confirmed current task is .ralph/tasks/story-04-observability/task-02-prometheus-metrics.md and prior turn moved its plan to NOW EXECUTE, so this turn is expected to implement unless the design is invalid on re-read.
- Next: read the task file, inspect the plan and boundary-smell notes, then execute in vertical TDD slices or revert to TO BE VERIFIED if the design no longer holds.

- Re-read the execution plan and the most relevant improve-code-boundaries smells: wrong-place knowledge, display boundary over string soup, and mixed responsibilities.
- Inspected httpserver, benchmarkrun coordinator/results, and benchrunner stats/runtime. The planned seam still holds: coordinator can expose a typed metrics snapshot/render boundary while httpserver stays transport-only.
- Next: add the first failing /metrics HTTP test, then introduce the new benchmarkrun metrics contract and thread histogram data through it in strict vertical TDD slices.

- Implemented the Prometheus metrics boundary in benchmarkrun and httpserver: added a typed MetricsSnapshot renderer in benchmarkrun, a thin /metrics HTTP handler, and widened the runner/coordinator contract so Prometheus histogram data comes from runtime state rather than JSON scraping.
- Added TDD coverage around /metrics transport/content-type, prefixed metric names, and histogram output/low-cardinality label behavior; also added a benchrunner stats test proving histogram buckets/count/sum are exported in seconds.
- Next: run the focused package suites, then the required repo gates (make check, make lint, make test), and if they pass update the task metadata, switch task, commit, and push.
